schema_doc: See README. Each riddle: {id, type, question, answer, options?, correctAnswer?, compatible_templates, format_hint?, difficulty, tags, source}

riddles.question: A man walks outside in heavy rain with no umbrella, no hat, and no raincoat. Yet not a single hair on his head gets wet. How?
riddles.answer: He's bald. No hair to get wet.

riddles.question: You're driving a school bus. At stop 1, four people board. At stop 2, eight board and two get off. At stop 3, three get off and four board. What color are the bus driver's eyes?
riddles.answer: Whatever color YOUR eyes are. You are the driver.

riddles.question: A father and his son are in an accident. The father dies on the spot. The son is rushed to the hospital. The surgeon looks at him and says, I can't operate on him — he's my son. How?
riddles.answer: The surgeon is his mother.

riddles.question: Two friends drink the same juice from the same jug. One dies, the other is fine. The poison was in the drink. How did one survive?
riddles.answer: The poison was in the ice cubes. One drank fast, the ice didn't melt. The other sipped slowly.

riddles.question: A man checks into a hotel on the 10th floor. He jumps out of his window and lands completely unharmed. How?
riddles.answer: He jumped into the swimming pool below.

riddles.question: A woman shoots her husband, then holds him underwater for five minutes. Minutes later, they go out for dinner. How?
riddles.answer: She's a photographer. She took his photo and developed it in a darkroom.

riddles.question: Four people need to cross a dark bridge. They have one torch. The bridge holds only two at a time. They take 1, 2, 5, and 10 minutes to cross. They have only 17 minutes total. How do they all make it?
riddles.answer: 1+2 cross (2 min). 1 returns (1 min). 5+10 cross (10 min). 2 returns (2 min). 1+2 cross (2 min). Total 17 minutes.

riddles.question: Two girls are born to the same mother, on the same day, same year, same hospital. But they are NOT twins. How?
riddles.answer: They are two of triplets. Or quadruplets.

riddles.question: Three friends pay 300 rupees for a hotel room, 100 each. The manager refunds 50. The bellboy keeps 20 and gives 10 back, three each. So they paid 90 each, totaling 270, plus the bellboy's 20 equals 290. Where's the missing 10?
riddles.answer: There is no missing 10. The 270 already includes the bellboy's 20. It's a misdirection — adding the 20 to 270 is the wrong calculation.

riddles.question: A man lives on the 20th floor. Every morning he takes the lift straight down. When he returns, he takes the lift to the 14th floor and walks up six flights — except on rainy days, when he goes straight to the 20th. Why?
riddles.answer: He's short. He can only reach button 14. On rainy days, he uses his umbrella to press the higher button 20.

riddles.question: You have three switches in the basement. One bulb is in the attic. You can only go upstairs once. How do you find the right switch?
riddles.answer: Turn one on for 5 minutes, off, turn the second on, then go up. Warm bulb = first. Lit bulb = second. Cold and off = third.
options: B. Turn one on for 5 mins, off, turn second on, go up and touch the bulb

riddles.question: A rooster sits on a triangular roof. It lays an egg. Which side does the egg roll down?
riddles.answer: Roosters don't lay eggs. Hens do.

riddles.question: You're in a race and overtake the person in 2nd place. What position are you in now?
riddles.answer: 2nd. You took their position, not 1st.

riddles.question: A man is found dead in a locked room. There's a pool of water and broken glass on the floor. No windows. No other exits. What happened?
riddles.answer: His fish tank broke. The fish died. He died of grief — or in some versions, the fish was his dying loved one. Lateral logic puzzle.
options: B. His fish tank broke and he died of grief
C. Someone threw water at him

riddles.question: In 1990, a man is 15 years old. In 1995, he is 10 years old. How is this possible?
riddles.answer: He was born in 2005 BC. The years are counting down.
options: B. He was born in 2005 BC
C. He lied about his age

riddles.question: How many months have 28 days?
riddles.answer: All 12. Every month has at least 28 days.

riddles.question: A plane crashes on the India-Pakistan border. Where do they bury the survivors?
riddles.answer: You don't bury survivors.

riddles.question: If you have me, you want to share me. If you share me, you no longer have me. What am I?
riddles.answer: A secret.

riddles.question: A girl fell off a 20-foot ladder but didn't get hurt. How?
riddles.answer: She fell from the first rung — barely off the ground.
options: A. She fell on a mattress
B. She was wearing a helmet
C. She fell from the first rung

riddles.question: Two guards stand at two doors. One door leads to heaven, one to hell. One guard always lies, the other always tells the truth. What ONE question do you ask to find the right door?
riddles.answer: Ask either: 'What would the OTHER guard say is the heaven door?' Both will point to hell. Pick the opposite door.
options: B. What would the other guard say is the heaven door?
D. Is it safe to go through your door?

riddles.question: A man is found dead in the middle of a desert with an unopened package next to him. If he had opened the package, he'd still be alive. What's in the package?
riddles.answer: A parachute. He fell from a plane.

riddles.question: A man is found hanging in an empty room. There's a puddle of water beneath his feet. No furniture, no ladder. How did he do it?
riddles.answer: He stood on a block of ice that melted.

riddles.question: A man walks into a restaurant and orders albatross soup. He takes one sip, walks outside, and shoots himself. Why?
riddles.answer: He survived a shipwreck. To save him, his crew told him they'd been eating albatross. Tasting real albatross now, he realized they had actually been feeding him his dead wife.

riddles.question: Antony and Cleopatra are found dead on the floor of a villa. There's broken glass and a small pool of water. They have no wounds. How did they die?
riddles.answer: They are goldfish. The cat knocked over their bowl.

riddles.question: A truck gets stuck under a low bridge. Engineers and police can't figure out how to free it. A 10-year-old boy walks up and solves it instantly. What did he say?
riddles.answer: Let some air out of the tires.

riddles.question: A man pushes his car to a hotel and tells the owner he's bankrupt. Why?
riddles.answer: He's playing Monopoly. He landed on the hotel.

riddles.question: A woman gives birth to two boys on the same day, in the same year, with the same mother and father — but they are not twins. How?
riddles.answer: They are two of a set of triplets.

riddles.question: A man calls his wife from a payphone. After they speak, he walks 50 feet and is killed by a falling grand piano. The police know it was murder. How?
riddles.answer: He was on a payphone in a building lobby. The killer dropped the piano from above — the call was a setup to keep him in place.

riddles.question: A man receives a package containing a single, perfect rose. He opens the card. It says only: 'Sorry.' He immediately calls the police. Why?
riddles.answer: He's the killer. The rose came from his victim's florist — a coded message that someone knows.

riddles.question: Romeo and Juliet are found dead on the floor in a puddle of water with broken glass. Nobody is in the house. How did they die?
riddles.answer: They are fish. Their bowl was knocked over.

riddles.question: A man pushes a car. It stops at a hotel and he loses all his money. He's also not driving. What is he doing?
riddles.answer: Playing Monopoly.

riddles.question: A man dies of suffocation. His body is found alone in a sealed wooden room with no doors and no windows. There's nothing around him except sawdust. How did he die?
riddles.answer: He was a magician trapped in a magic box that didn't open from inside.

riddles.question: A woman walks into a bar and asks the bartender for a glass of water. The bartender pulls out a gun and points it at her. The woman thanks him and leaves. Why?
riddles.answer: She had hiccups. The bartender scared them away with the gun.

riddles.question: A man sits in a restaurant. He orders a steak. After tasting it, he runs outside and jumps off a cliff. Why?
riddles.answer: Years earlier his ship sank. The crew survived by eating what they said was beef. Tasting real steak now, he realized they had eaten people.

riddles.question: A woman is shot in the head while sitting in her car. The doors are locked from the inside. The windows are up. There's no gun in the car. How was she killed?
riddles.answer: It's a convertible — the top was down.

riddles.question: A man enters a house. There's no electricity. He turns on a candle, a fireplace, and an oil lamp. He has only one match. Which does he light first?
riddles.answer: The match.

riddles.question: What can travel around the world while staying in the same corner?
riddles.answer: A postage stamp.

riddles.question: I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have nobody, but come alive with the wind. What am I?
riddles.answer: An echo.

riddles.question: What gets wetter the more it dries?
riddles.answer: A towel.

riddles.question: The more of this there is, the less you see. What is it?
riddles.answer: Darkness.

riddles.question: A detective walks into a room. A man lies dead on the floor next to a small puddle and a piece of broken glass. The detective immediately knows it was murder. How?
riddles.answer: There's no water on the man — meaning he didn't break the glass himself. Someone else cleaned up after.

riddles.question: A man is pushed off a 50-story building. He survives without a scratch. How?
riddles.answer: He was pushed off the inside — a window-cleaner's lift platform was below him.

riddles.question: A woman left her apartment to go shopping. When she came back, her husband was dead with a knife in his back. She didn't call the police but instead made dinner. Why?
riddles.answer: Her husband was a butcher. The knife had been in his back from work — she didn't notice it was anything new.

riddles.question: A man jumps out of a plane with no parachute. He lands safely. How?
riddles.answer: The plane was on the ground.

riddles.question: What has a head, a tail, but no body?
riddles.answer: A coin.

riddles.question: A man calls a hotel to book a room. The clerk says the room costs 100 a night. He pays cash. He leaves before sunrise without checking out. The hotel reports him as a thief. Why?
riddles.answer: The 100 he paid was counterfeit. The clerk had given him real change.

riddles.question: A woman is found dead in her bedroom. Her last text said 'I'm fine, just tired.' Police rule it murder. How did they know?
riddles.answer: She was deaf. Her phone was set to vibrate-only — but the message used a voice-to-text feature that requires hearing. Someone else sent it.

riddles.question: A 911 call comes in. The caller says only: 'It's coming from inside the house.' Then silence. Police trace the call. The line was disconnected the day before. How?
riddles.answer: The caller was using a landline plugged into a working jack — but the bill was unpaid. Disconnect-pending lines still allow emergency calls.

riddles.question: A woman is found dead in a snowy field. There are no footprints leading to her body. No tire marks. No tracks of any kind. How did she get there?
riddles.answer: She fell from a plane — or jumped. The wind covered any nearby evidence.

riddles.question: Two fathers and two sons go fishing. Each catches one fish. They come home with only three fish. Why?
riddles.answer: There were three people: a grandfather, a father, and a son. The father is both a father and a son.
options: C. There were only three people: grandfather, father, son

riddles.question: Music stops and a woman dies. Why?
riddles.answer: She is a blindfolded tightrope walker. The music was her cue to know she had reached the safety platform. The music stopped early — she stepped off into thin air.

riddles.question: A man rode into town on Friday. He stayed for three days and left on Friday. How?
riddles.answer: His horse's name was Friday.

riddles.question: Two men are playing chess. They play 5 games. Each man wins 3 games. How?
riddles.answer: They are not playing each other — they are playing different opponents.

riddles.question: A man is found dead outside a building. He has a rock in his hand and broken glass nearby. What happened?
riddles.answer: He was trapped inside a sinking vehicle in a river — used the rock to break the window and escape, but didn't survive the current.

riddles.question: A man gets into an elevator. When it stops, he knows his wife is dead. How?
riddles.answer: His wife is on life support at the hospital. The elevator lost power — meaning the hospital's electricity failed, stopping the life-support machine.

riddles.question: A man is running down a corridor. The lights flicker and he drops to his knees and cries. Why?
riddles.answer: He is a messenger running a pardon to stop an execution. The flickering lights mean the electric chair has just been used — he arrived too late.

riddles.question: Beulah died in the Appalachians while Craig died at sea. Everyone was happier with Craig's death. Why?
riddles.answer: Beulah and Craig were hurricanes. Craig's path at sea caused far less damage than Beulah's.

riddles.question: A man lies dead in a field with a match near his outstretched hand. What happened?
riddles.answer: He was in a failing hot air balloon. Passengers drew matches to decide who would jump. He drew the short match and jumped.

riddles.question: A man runs into a fire and lives. A man stays where there is no fire and dies. How?
riddles.answer: A CO2 fire suppression system activated in the room where the second man stayed — removing all oxygen. The first man ran through the fire with minor burns and survived.

riddles.question: A body is found in Chicago in summer with a fractured skull, broken bones, and hypothermia as cause of death. How?
riddles.answer: He stowed away in a plane's landing gear. He died of hypothermia at altitude, fell when the gear opened on approach, and landed in the city.

riddles.question: A man tells his boss: 'I dreamed your plane would crash — don't fly today.' His boss immediately fires him. Why?
riddles.answer: He is the night watchman. He was dreaming on the job — asleep instead of keeping watch.

riddles.question: A man is driving and turns on the radio. He listens for 5 minutes, then turns around and drives home — and shoots his wife. Why?
riddles.answer: He heard a news bulletin describing an armed fugitive at large — and realised the description matched the man he had left alone at home with his wife.

riddles.question: A travel agent reads a newspaper article about a woman who fell overboard at sea — and immediately knows her husband murdered her. How?
riddles.answer: He had sold the husband two tickets for the voyage — one round-trip and one one-way. The wife's name matched the one-way ticket.

riddles.question: A physics professor demonstrates conservation of energy with a bowling ball on a rope — releasing it from his nose. He ends up in hospital. Why?
riddles.answer: He gave the ball a slight push instead of a clean release — so it swung back with extra force and hit his nose.

riddles.question: A married couple goes to a drive-in movie. During the film, the husband strangles his wife. Nobody notices. How?
riddles.answer: They were at a drive-in theatre — alone inside their car. Everyone else was watching the screen.

riddles.question: A man is returning from Switzerland by train. If he had been in a non-smoking compartment, he would have died. How?
riddles.answer: He is blind; his cane was stolen. In the smoking car, other passengers warned him not to open the wrong door at a cliff-side station stop. In the non-smoking car, no one would have warned him.

riddles.question: A woman calls police about the murderer next door. Police arrest the man — then charge the woman. Why?
riddles.answer: She had known for some time before calling — making her liable for harbouring a fugitive.

riddles.question: A woman who has been blind her whole life is given her sight. She laughs, then cries, then laughs again. Why?
riddles.answer: She laughs at the beauty of the world, cries when she sees how old she looks in a mirror, then laughs again when she sees the faces of people she loves for the first time.

riddles.question: A man is found dead at the bottom of a building, holding a small piece of straw. What happened?
riddles.answer: He and others were trapped on the roof after a fire. They drew straws to decide who would jump to signal rescuers. He drew the short straw.

riddles.question: Jenny is found dead in her car. Police contact her husband. He rushes directly to the scene and is immediately arrested. Why?
riddles.answer: Police never told him where the car was — yet he went straight to it. Only the killer would know.

riddles.question: What word starts with E, ends with E, and contains only one letter?
riddles.answer: Envelope. It starts and ends with 'e' — and contains a letter inside.

riddles.question: A word I know — six letters it contains. Subtract just one, and twelve remains. What is it?
riddles.answer: Dozens. Remove the 's' → dozen = twelve.

riddles.question: Saturday and Sunday I am big. Tuesday through Thursday I am small. Monday and Friday I don't exist at all. What am I?
riddles.answer: The letter S. Present in Saturday and Sunday, once in Tuesday–Thursday, absent from Monday and Friday.

riddles.question: I am feared by many. Scramble my letters and I become hated. What am I?
riddles.answer: Death. DEATH is an anagram of HATED.

riddles.question: Before Mount Everest was discovered, what was the highest mountain in the world?
riddles.answer: Mount Everest — it was still the tallest. We just hadn't discovered it yet.
options: C. Mount Everest — it existed before its discovery
D. The highest known mountain at the time

riddles.question: How can the number four be half of five?
riddles.answer: Write FIVE in Roman numerals: IV. The first half — IV — equals 4.

riddles.question: The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
riddles.answer: Footsteps.

riddles.question: I have cities but no houses. I have mountains but no trees. I have water but no fish. I have roads but no cars. What am I?
riddles.answer: A map.

riddles.question: I am always in front of you but can never be seen. What am I?
riddles.answer: The future.

riddles.question: John's mother has three sons. The first is named Penny. The second is named Nickel. What is the third son's name?
riddles.answer: John — the question told you at the start.

riddles.question: A snail climbs a 10-metre pole. Each day it climbs 3 metres; each night it slides back 2 metres. How many days to reach the top?
riddles.answer: 8 days. After 7 days it is at 7 metres. On day 8 it climbs to 10 — and doesn't slide back.

riddles.question: A man is 20 years old but has only had 5 birthdays. How?
riddles.answer: He was born on February 29th — a leap day that occurs once every four years.

riddles.question: A woman who had never met her sister-in-law instantly recognises her across a crowded room. How?
riddles.answer: Her sister-in-law looks exactly like her husband — they are identical twins.

riddles.question: A man is cleaning windows on the 25th floor and slips and falls — yet suffers no injuries. How?
riddles.answer: He was cleaning them from the inside.

riddles.question: A man is found dead in a phone box. The glass is shattered outward from inside. No blood, no weapon. What happened?
riddles.answer: Lightning struck the phone box while he was on the call. The electrocution killed him and the force shattered the glass outward.

riddles.question: A man returns from a coastal holiday completely sunburned — but was never outside during daylight. How?
riddles.answer: He worked night shifts in a lighthouse. The rotating beam repeatedly swept over him inside.

riddles.question: Two boxers fight a 12-round bout. One wins every round on the scorecards — but the other is declared the overall winner. No draw, no error, no DQ. How?
riddles.answer: They are fighting different opponents in separate bouts — not against each other.

riddles.question: A man dials emergency services but orders a pizza instead of speaking. The dispatcher sends police. How did she know?
riddles.answer: The dispatcher recognised a covert distress protocol — someone threatening him was nearby. He couldn't speak freely.

riddles.question: A man goes on a trip with a suitcase. He opens it at his destination — and immediately kills himself. Why?
riddles.answer: He is a circus knife-thrower's assistant. The suitcase contained the knives. They are gone — his partner performed the act with a replacement. He couldn't bear being replaced.

riddles.question: A woman walks into a library and asks for books on paranoia. The librarian whispers: 'They're right behind you.' Why does she whisper?
riddles.answer: She's a librarian — she whispers because that's the rule in a library.

riddles.question: A man riding a horse can't stop — the horse keeps going. Yet the man is perfectly calm. Why?
riddles.answer: The horse is on a carousel.

riddles.question: An old lady always answers the door in her hat and coat. Why?
riddles.answer: If she wants to invite you in she says she just got back. If she doesn't want to see you she says she's just going out.

riddles.question: A man is found dead in a car with the windows up and doors locked from inside. No crash, no violence. What happened?
riddles.answer: He parked in a garage with the engine running and died of carbon monoxide poisoning.

riddles.question: A man's house collapses on him in his sleep. It was caused by the weather — yet he was not ill, poisoned, or attacked. How?
riddles.answer: His house was an igloo. A warm spell melted it while he slept.

riddles.question: One woman received a human arm in the mail, examined it, and sent it to another woman. That woman also examined it — and buried it. Why?
riddles.answer: Three people were on a mountain expedition and got lost. One lost an arm in an accident. The other two survived by eating it. The arm was later returned — each examined it to confirm what they had done, then it was given a proper burial.

riddles.question: Hans and Fritz are spies posing as American tourists. Hans is arrested at the border; Fritz is not. What gave Hans away?
riddles.answer: Hans spoke English without an authentic American accent. Fritz had genuinely lived in the US and spoke naturally.

riddles.question: A fish's head is 3 cm. Its tail is the same as the head plus half the body. The body is as long as the head and tail combined. How long is the fish?
riddles.answer: 24 cm. Head = 3, Body = 12, Tail = 9. Total = 24.

riddles.question: You have two ropes and a lighter. Each rope burns in exactly one hour but not evenly. How do you measure exactly 45 minutes?
riddles.answer: Light both ends of rope 1 and one end of rope 2 simultaneously. When rope 1 burns out (30 min), light rope 2's other end. It burns out in 15 more minutes. Total = 45 min.

riddles.question: Game show: 3 doors — one car, two goats. You pick a door. Host opens another to reveal a goat. Should you switch?
riddles.answer: Yes. Staying gives 1/3 odds; switching gives 2/3 odds of winning the car.
options: B. Switch — your odds improve to 2/3
D. Switch only if host hesitated

riddles.question: A farmer must cross a river with a wolf, a goat, and a cabbage. The boat holds only him and one other. Left alone: wolf eats goat; goat eats cabbage. How does he get all three across?
riddles.answer: Take goat over. Return alone. Take wolf over. Bring goat back. Take cabbage over. Return alone. Take goat over.

riddles.question: A man shows a portrait and says: 'Brothers and sisters I have none, but that man's father is my father's son.' Whose portrait is it?
riddles.answer: His son. 'My father's son' with no siblings = himself. So 'that man's father is me.'

riddles.question: Five people eat apples. A finishes before B but behind C. D finishes before E but behind B. What is the finishing order?
riddles.answer: CABDE.

riddles.question: There are two ducks in front of a duck, two ducks behind a duck, and one in the middle. How many ducks total?
riddles.answer: Three — lined up in a row.

riddles.question: A sloth climbs 6 feet up a 30-foot pole each day and slides back 5 feet each night. How many days to reach the top?
riddles.answer: 25 days. After 24 days it's at 24 feet. On day 25 it climbs to 30 and stops.

riddles.question: If 11 + 2 = 1, what does 9 + 5 equal?
riddles.answer: 2. Clock arithmetic: 11 o'clock + 2 hours = 1 o'clock; 9 o'clock + 5 hours = 2 o'clock.

riddles.question: When will 5 plus 2 equal 1?
riddles.answer: When telling time — 5 o'clock plus 2 hours = 7 o'clock, or 5 days plus 2 = 1 week.

riddles.question: If it takes 2 men 2 days to dig 2 holes, how long will it take 4 men to dig half a hole?
riddles.answer: You can't dig half a hole — a hole is either dug or it isn't.

riddles.question: If I had 4 eggs and a thief gave me 3, and my rooster laid 5 more — how many eggs do I have?
riddles.answer: 3 eggs. 'If I had' is hypothetical (start at zero). Thief gave 3 (real). Roosters don't lay eggs.

riddles.question: Forward I'm what you do every day. Backwards I'm something you hate. What am I?
riddles.answer: Live. Forwards: live. Backwards: evil.

riddles.question: Two in a corner, one in a room, zero in a house, but one in a shelter. What am I?
riddles.answer: The letter R. coRneR (2), Room (1), house (0), shelteR (1).

riddles.question: When it is alive we sing. When it is dead we clap our hands. What is it?
riddles.answer: A birthday candle. We sing Happy Birthday while it burns — clap when it's blown out.

riddles.question: When I change I get bigger but weigh less. What am I?
riddles.answer: Popcorn. Kernels expand massively in size while losing moisture when heated.

riddles.question: What do you buy to eat but never consume?
riddles.answer: Cutlery. You buy it to eat with — but never eat the cutlery itself.

riddles.question: A bus driver goes the wrong way down a one-way street, past two cops. They don't stop him. Why?
riddles.answer: He was walking — not driving a bus.

riddles.question: What has branches but no trunk, leaves, or fruit?
riddles.answer: A bank. Banks have branches — but none of the botanical kind.

riddles.question: You see me once in June, twice in November, and not at all in May. What am I?
riddles.answer: The letter E. junE (1), novEmbEr (2), may (0).

riddles.question: What has two words but thousands of letters?
riddles.answer: The post office — two words in the name, thousands of actual letters inside.

riddles.question: What word in English has its first 2 letters meaning a male, first 3 meaning a female, first 4 meaning great, and the whole word meaning a great woman?
riddles.answer: Heroine. He = male, her = female, hero = great, heroine = great woman.

riddles.question: What is 3/7 chicken, 2/3 cat, and 2/4 goat?
riddles.answer: Chicago. CHI (3/7 of CHIcken) + CA (2/3 of CAt) + GO (2/4 of GOat) = CHICAGO.

riddles.question: What five-letter word stays the same when you take away the first, third, and last letter?
riddles.answer: Empty. Remove E, P, Y → MT (em-tee) — still sounds like 'empty.'

riddles.question: I am a 5-letter word. Remove the first letter and I become a country. Remove the first two and I become the opposite of what I was. What am I?
riddles.answer: Woman. Remove W → Oman. Remove Wo → Man.

riddles.question: What only works the first time you use it?
riddles.answer: A match.

riddles.question: Which side of a turkey has the most feathers?
riddles.answer: The outside.

riddles.question: Open me and you can't see me without a mirror. Close me and you can't see me at all. What am I?
riddles.answer: Your eyes.

riddles.question: What breaks yet never falls, and what falls yet never breaks?
riddles.answer: Day breaks (dawn) but never falls. Night falls but never breaks.

riddles.question: What goes through cities and fields but never moves?
riddles.answer: A road.

riddles.question: What tastes better than it smells?
riddles.answer: Your tongue — it tastes but has no smell of its own.

riddles.question: Poor people have it. Rich people need it. If you eat it you die. What is it?
riddles.answer: Nothing. Poor people have nothing, rich people need nothing, eat nothing and you starve.

riddles.question: I can only live where there is light, but I die if the light shines on me. What am I?
riddles.answer: A shadow.

riddles.question: What can the left hand hold that the right hand never can?
riddles.answer: Your right elbow.

riddles.question: What can you hold in your right hand but not in your left?
riddles.answer: Your left hand — or your left elbow.

riddles.question: What word, if said wrong, is right — and if said right, is wrong?
riddles.answer: The word 'wrong.'

riddles.question: A man is wearing all black and walks at night with no streetlights on. A black car with no headlights drives toward him — yet easily avoids him. How?
riddles.answer: It is daytime. No lights needed.

riddles.question: I don't have eyes but once I did see. Once I had thoughts but now I'm white and empty. What am I?
riddles.answer: A skull.

riddles.question: A man shaves several times a day yet still has a beard. Who is he?
riddles.answer: A barber. He shaves other people all day but keeps his own beard.

riddles.question: What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
riddles.answer: A teapot. Begins with T, ends with t, and has tea in it.

riddles.question: Mary was born on December 25th but her birthday is always in the summer. How?
riddles.answer: Mary lives in the Southern Hemisphere, where December is summer.

riddles.question: A guard says: 'Tell the truth and I'll kill you with my sword. Tell a lie and I'll kill you with my spell.' What do you say to survive?
riddles.answer: 'You will kill me with your spell.' This creates a paradox — he can do neither. You survive.

riddles.question: Five pieces of coal, a carrot, and a scarf are lying on a lawn. Nobody put them there. What happened?
riddles.answer: Children built a snowman. The snow melted — leaving the coal eyes, carrot nose, and scarf behind.

riddles.question: A man in Heaven identifies one couple among thousands of naked people as Adam and Eve. How?
riddles.answer: They were the only two without belly buttons — never having been born of a woman.

riddles.question: A man on a railway bridge sees a fast train approaching. He runs 10 feet TOWARD the train before jumping to safety. Why?
riddles.answer: Running toward the train got him off the bridge faster — onto solid ground where he could jump to safety. Running away would have kept him on the bridge longer.

riddles.question: A hiker finds a fallen road sign at an intersection with no map or phone — yet correctly re-erects it. How?
riddles.answer: He knew which town he had just come from. He pointed that arrow back toward his origin — automatically orienting all others correctly.

riddles.question: There are a dozen eggs in a carton. Twelve people each take one egg but one egg remains. How?
riddles.answer: The last person took the carton with the egg still inside it.

riddles.question: A boat has a ladder hanging off the side with 3 feet submerged. The tide rises 5 feet. How much ladder is underwater?
riddles.answer: Still 3 feet — the boat rises with the tide.

riddles.question: Which of these countries does NOT have a 4th of July: USA, UK, France, Germany, Australia, Canada?
riddles.answer: None — every country has a 4th of July on the calendar. Only the USA celebrates it as a holiday.

riddles.question: Police raid a house for a man named John. Inside they find a carpenter, lorry driver, mechanic, and fireman playing poker. They immediately arrest the fireman. Why?
riddles.answer: The other three are women. The fireman is the only man — so he must be John.

riddles.question: Every two weeks a woman sits down and writes exactly two words on 60 sheets of paper. Why?
riddles.answer: She is signing her name — writing paycheques for 60 employees.

riddles.question: A girl celebrated her birthday. Two days later, her older twin brother celebrated his birthday. How?
riddles.answer: She was born just before midnight on October 31st; he was born just after midnight on November 1st — two days later on the calendar.

riddles.question: A newspaper reports a woman died falling off a mountain in the Alps. A reader immediately knows her husband murdered her. How?
riddles.answer: He is the travel agent who sold the husband two tickets — one round-trip and one one-way. Only the husband was coming back.
